published_prior_art.cite: RambachanRoth2023 + KimKwonKwonLee2018: support-function/smoothness partial-ID machinery exists, but not for continuous-DID ACR support gaps
none; verified continuous-DID papers point identify or use no-stayer/quasi-control/parametric routes, not sharp C^{1,1} derivative envelopes
DHaultfoeuilleHoderleinSasaki2023 and deChaisemartinDHaultfoeuillePasquierSowVazquezBare2022: point-ID continuous-DID limits under their support primitives

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The survey misses Kim, Kwon, Kwon, and Lee (2018, Quantitative Economics), a close smoothness-based partial-identification paper directly relevant to monotone/smooth response bounds.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship unless the redraft quotes an explicit open support-gap problem in the no-stayer continuous-DID literature and states a strictly new sharp-bound object beyond generic smoothness interpolation.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The finite-grid feasible set omits value-derivative compatibility/Taylor integral constraints, and the 'derivative coordinate at the grid cell containing d' is undefined unless a variable for m'(d) or an interpolation convention is added.

soundness_flags.label: Corollary homogeneous linear dose response
soundness_flags.one_line: Two singleton endpoint values plus a true linear μ do not make B_gap a singleton; the identified set ranges over all compatible monotone C^{1,1} curves unless linearity is imposed on the admissible class or B=0/dense support is added.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add the missing C^{1,1} interpolation constraints linking m_j, v_j, and m'(d): e.g. Taylor upper/lower inequalities or an explicit Whitney-compatibility system, then restate Theorem 1 as a genuine projection.
Replace the homogeneous-linear corollary with a valid boundary case: B=0, dense singleton support near d, or an explicit additional assumption that the admissible response class is affine.

Add KimKwonKwonLee2018 and smoothness/beyond-overlap partial-ID papers to Related work, and explain why the DID moment source plus derivative target is not just their smoothness-bound argument with relabelled support points.

To meet the flagship floor, quote a specific open limitation from deChaisemartinDHaultfoeuilleVazquezBare2024 or deChaisemartinCicciaDHaultfoeuilleKnau2024 and sharpen Conjecture 1 into a closed-form, necessary-and-sufficient modulus that strictly improves a named fallback.

recommended_next_step: Revise the mathematical statement first, then redo the published-axis comparison against smoothness partial-identification literature before resubmitting for flagship review.
